settings.title: Packaging Line for Rootcellar Ferments
settings.summary: We designed a label family for a fermentation brand expanding from four products to twelve.
settings.description:
Rootcellar started with four jars sold at a farmers market and was heading into grocery with a dozen products and no consistent look. The original labels had been made one at a time, which was fine for a market stall but fell apart on a shelf next to established brands.

The brief

The founders were about to triple their range and knew they would keep adding products after that. They did not want to call a designer for every new flavor. The job was a label family that looked like one brand and that they could extend on their own.

What we did

We created a label system with a shared layout that stays the same across every product, so the family reads as a set the moment you see two jars together. Within that layout, a clear flavor color key tells each variety apart at a glance, on the shelf and in the fridge.

  • A single shared label layout for the whole range
  • A flavor color key that distinguishes each product
  • Artwork rules for adding new products without us
  • Print-ready files sized for their jars

The artwork rules were the part that mattered most for a growing brand. We documented exactly how a new label is built, which colors are in play, and where the type and key sit, so the team can launch a thirteenth and fourteenth product that still belong to the family.

The result

The full line hit grocery shelves looking like one family rather than a dozen one-off jars. A regional grocer picked up the range after the redesign, and the color key means a shopper can find their flavor without reading every label.
